header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary:
I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Program Enablement professional who thrives on curating learning resources through research and collaboration, aligning curriculum with learner needs, and improving programs through data analysis. 
Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve learner experience—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Curate {prog|learning resources} via vendor research and instructor collaboration, contributing to the team's highest B2B revenue of $316K
bullets.original: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
bullets.reason: NEW bullet — curation work + B2B revenue evidence; differentiates from bullet 2 (vendor lifecycle) by leading with business outcome

bullets.text: Partner with 6+ global vendors on {prog|build-or-buy decisions}, developing and iterating 15+ {prog|educational products} across the {coord|product lifecycle}
bullets.original: Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
bullets.reason: Emphasizes vendor collaboration over 'sourcing' and reflects Jamie's actual lifecycle work (develop, select, iterate) — avoids overstated 'retirement' framing

bullets.text: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ {cross|stakeholders} to {prog|restructure curriculum} for 3 {prog|educational products} aligned with student needs
bullets.original: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
bullets.reason: Replaced 'design 3 new programs' with 'restructure 3 educational products' — more accurate to Jamie's actual work and maps to JD's 'redevelopment and continuous quality improvement cycles'

bullets.text: Develop guides and {coord|resource documentation} for 70+ staff and facilitate training for 600+ participants, reducing onboarding by 75%
bullets.original: Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
bullets.reason: Adds 'resource documentation' framing for JD's centralized asset tracking + facilitation language for stakeholder collaboration requirement

bullets.text: Provided {prog|consultations} to improve {prog|internal training programs} on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
bullets.reason: Reordered to lead — internal training program development is most relevant to WGU's learning resource and curriculum focus

bullets.text: Improved people experience via 5 cross-functional projects focusing on L&D, survey development, and rewards & recognition
bullets.original: Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
bullets.reason: Moved up; reordered list to lead with L&D; swapped 'employee experience' to 'learner experience' to mirror WGU framing

bullets.text: Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
bullets.reason: Moved down — relevant for data analysis but less directly tied to learning resource curation

bullets.text: Analyzed 2,000+ people experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
bullets.reason: Moved down — strong data analytics signal but least tied to program/curriculum work

bullets.text: Partnered with HRBP team to {prog|design and pilot} an {prog|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
bullets.original: Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
bullets.reason: Reframed lead bullet to emphasize program design and pilot — directly maps to WGU's program development and learning resource scaling

bullets.text: Optimized {coord|onboarding processes} across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
bullets.reason: Moved up — process documentation and lifecycle work parallels WGU's centralized asset tracking system

bullets.text: Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
bullets.reason: Moved down — strong stakeholder coordination signal but less tied to learning resource work

bullets.text: Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants
bullets.reason: Moved to lead — university partnerships and student-facing engagement is closest tie to WGU's higher-education context

bullets.text: Filled a challenging international role by drafting {prog|job descriptions}, posting openings, and researching global compensation
bullets.reason: Moved up — content drafting and research is the closest learning-resource adjacency in this role

bullets.text: Engaged global talent via personalized {cross|communication} and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
bullets.reason: Held middle position — stakeholder engagement signal

bullets.text: Developed proficiency across HR functions spanning recruitment, compensation, and performance management
bullets.reason: Moved to last — broadest/least specific bullet

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)
bullets: Relevant Coursework: Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Learning and Motivation, Psychological Testing

projects: OD Consulting: Partner with Organization Development Network (ODN) Oregon on client-facing engagements — manage projects with executive leadership, coach HR teams in data analytics, and inform organizational change
Consulting Projects: Informed Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design/execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube via mixed-method research
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)
skills: Technical Skills: LMS (Canvas, Moodle) · Project Management (Asana, Airtable) · Knowledge Management (Notion, SharePoint, Google Workspace) · Design Tools (Canva, Adobe InDesign) · Data Analysis (SPSS, Excel) · GenAI Tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude)

jd.meta: WGU — Learning Resource Strategist · Salt Lake City, UT · $49,800–$74,600 · JR-025002

sections.title: Role Overview
sections.content: Designs, develops, and optimizes {prog|learning resources} across academic programs. Ensures students access best-in-class, strategically sourced {prog|learning materials}. Provides {coord|high-level oversight and management} of the {coord|learning resource lifecycle}, from program ideation through ongoing quality improvement. Applies expertise in {prog|content strategy}, {prog|resource evaluation}, and market trends.

items.text: {prog|Evaluate and curate} {prog|learning resources} aligned to course competencies
Manage {coord|full lifecycle} from selection, vendor onboarding, implementation, to retirement
Research and recommend new {prog|learning resources}; manage {coord|onboarding}
Lead {coord|learning resource request process} with {cross|stakeholders} (Procurement, PDOs, accessibility)
Ensure ADA accessibility & technical compatibility with WGU systems
Demonstrated experience selecting, evaluating, and curating learning resources beyond assigning pre-existing materials ✅
Identify and recommend labs, simulations, hands-on exercises ⚠️ (academic resources, not labs/sims directly)
Conducting research for instructional use, comparing resources from vendors ✅
Aligning learning resources to instructional objectives, course outcomes, student needs ✅
Bachelor's degree in Education, Instructional Design, Library Science, etc. ✅ (Education Studies)
Publishing, Instructional Design, Online Higher Ed, Library Science ⚠️ (no direct ID/publishing)
EdTech platforms and LMS ✅ (Canvas, Moodle exposure)
Accessibility standards for digital learning materials ⚠️ (limited)

sections.title: ✅ H1B Status
sections.content: WGU = accredited non-profit university → CAP-EXEMPT. No H1B lottery risk. Strong visa story.

changeLog.change: Reframed identity from 'Organizational Development professional' to 'Program Enablement professional' with emphasis on curating learning resources, vendor partnerships, and curriculum alignment — matches the WGU role's core function.

Added new bullet surfacing Jamie's actual InGenius work on evaluating/curating learning resources via vendor research and instructor collaboration, plus curriculum restructuring. Directly answers JD min-qual.
Reframed vendor-launch bullet to emphasize 'full product lifecycle from selection to retirement' — mirrors JD's lifecycle management language.
Word-level swaps to surface 'student needs' alignment and 'resource documentation' (asset tracking).

Added relevant coursework (Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Learning and Motivation, Psychological Testing) — directly maps to WGU's instructional design and adult learning context.
Led with LMS (Canvas, Moodle) per JD's preferred qualifications on educational technology platforms; preserved PM and KM tools.
Per Jamie's instruction, did NOT pull Mercer L&D vendor management framing into this resume. The bridge for academic learning resource roles is InGenius, not Mercer corporate L&D.

Per Jamie's instruction, removed ODN from Experience section and condensed to one Projects line — ODN's leave-audit work is tangentially relevant to academic curriculum/learning resource curation, so it lives better as a project credential than full work experience.

Per Jamie's feedback, replaced 'Source 6+ vendors' with 'Partner with 6+ global vendors' to emphasize collaboration; replaced 'selection to retirement' with 'develop, select, and iterate on' to honestly reflect Jamie's actual lifecycle work.
Reordered to lead with internal training program consultations (most relevant to learning programs), then L&D-focused cross-functional projects, then data work.

Lead bullet reframed to 'Partnered with HRBP team to design and pilot an Inclusive Leadership workshop' — emphasizes program design. Onboarding moved up (process documentation parallels asset tracking systems); M&A moved down.
Lead with university recruiting events bullet (closest tie to higher-ed context), then international JD drafting (content development), then engagement, then broad HR proficiency.
